tools.name: get_started
tools.description: Call this FIRST when a user has just connected or is unsure what Noemic is. The embedded panel presents marketplace context but has no action buttons. Present the four paths through Claude's native tappable choices; do not repeat the panel information.

tools.name: begin_sign_in
tools.description: Call only when the user is ready for an identity-bearing action. Returns a secure noemic.app URL where they log in or create an account and approve this conversation. Show only signInUrl; keep requestId and pollSecret private. Never ask for credentials in chat.

tools.name: finish_sign_in
tools.description: After the user chooses 'I've signed in', exchange the private requestId and pollSecret from begin_sign_in. Pending means they have not approved yet; complete returns the private sessionToken. Never expose either secret.

tools.name: logout
tools.description: Invalidate a sessionToken (e.g. on a shared device). After this the token no longer works and you must login again.

tools.name: match_me
tools.description: PRIVATE fallback pre-ranker when list_people is too large for context. Returns at most three candidates as working material and NEVER renders a panel. Read silently, then call show_people with 1-3 selected IDs. Do not narrate ranking or results.

tools.name: list_people
tools.description: PRIMARY PRIVATE DISCOVERY: first decide which formal factual constraints follow from the user's request, pass only those filters, then read every returned story and corpus silently. This tool NEVER renders to the user. Select 1-3 people and call show_people with the same filters. Never narrate the s

tools.name: show_people
tools.description: THE ONLY user-visible shortlist. After silently reading list_people or match_me, pass 1-3 selected profile IDs plus the formal filters you actually applied. The panel displays one person at a time with local name selectors. Then render one Claude-native choice per person and one escape choice; no pr

tools.name: get_person
tools.description: Full profile (story + corpus) for one person by profile id.

tools.name: get_availability
tools.description: Open (unbooked, future) slots for a person, soonest first.

tools.name: book_session
tools.description: Book an open slot with a person. Pricing is automatic: first-ever session on the platform is free; otherwise an available session credit auto-redeems; otherwise the pro's self-set rate is charged. The result states what was paid — tell the user.

tools.name: book_now
tools.description: Instant session with someone whose availableNow is true: the booking starts immediately and the result carries the roomUrl — send the user straight in. Same pricing as book_session (free first session where offered, then credits, then the prorated rate — state the price first). Default 30 minutes.

tools.name: set_available_now
tools.description: Pro-side presence has TWO parts: this controllable status (open/closed) AND recent activity — a pro shows as available-now only while their status is open AND they've been active in the last 15 minutes (their dashboard heartbeats while open; calling this tool also counts as activity). So: open it wh

tools.name: complete_session
tools.description: Call after a session actually happened. The PRO earns a session credit they can spend as a client (the loop that keeps Noemic alive) and their quality record grows.

tools.name: cancel_booking
tools.description: Cancel a confirmed booking: any charge is refunded, a redeemed credit is returned, and the slot reopens.

tools.name: my_bookings
tools.description: Bookings as client and as pro, soonest first.

tools.name: become_available
tools.description: Create the user's profile so others can book them. Draft everything from your conversation — their words, their experiences, what they'd be good at being in someone's corner for — and confirm with them before calling. No credentials needed, no review queue: the only qualification is being human. Rat

tools.name: my_profile
tools.description: Status probe: does the user have a profile, what's missing to make it bookable, plus credits, upcoming sessions, and active commitments at a glance. Call this before assuming anything about the user's state — cheaper than asking them.

tools.name: add_availability
tools.description: Add open session slots to the user's profile. endsAt defaults to one hour after startsAt.

tools.name: add_to_my_corpus
tools.description: Append text to the user's agent-readable corpus (with their consent). Anything that would help future matching: new experiences, what a session with them is like, boundaries, wins from sessions they ran. Append-only and date-stamped.

tools.name: update_my_profile
tools.description: Update display name, headline, story, rate, or listing/visibility flags.

tools.name: create_commitment
tools.description: The user stakes real money on doing a specific thing by a deadline. Succeed → refunded; fail → forfeited. Make the title concrete and checkable ('run 3x this week', not 'exercise more'). Follow up with the user before the deadline. Server-enforced cap: at most $500 of active stakes per user; the res

tools.name: resolve_commitment
tools.description: Resolve an active commitment: succeeded → stake refunded; failed → stake forfeited. Self-report — ask the user directly and record their answer honestly.

tools.name: my_commitments
tools.description: All commitments with status and deadlines (active first). Check for approaching deadlines worth nudging about.

tools.name: session_transcript
tools.description: The consent-gated transcript of a session in the platform room. Participants only. During a LIVE session, poll with sinceMs set to the atMs of the last segment you saw (only newer segments return). Use it to supervise: understand where the conversation is, then send_cue when one would help.

tools.name: send_cue
tools.description: Pro-only, during a live session. The cue appears quietly in the pro's room panel — the client never sees it. Discipline: ONE short cue (max 25 words), specific to what was actually said — a question worth asking, a dropped thread, a reminder to listen, a concrete next step. Silence is fine; never se

tools.name: write_debrief
tools.description: Pro-only, after a session. Read the full transcript first (session_transcript), then write honestly and specifically, referencing actual moments: strengths = 2-3 things that worked; growth = the single most important thing to do differently and where it mattered; drill = one concrete exercise before

tools.name: session_debrief
tools.description: After a session ends, the pro gets a private AI debrief (what worked, what to change, a drill to practice). Pro-only: the email must be the session's pro. Use it to help your user prepare for their next session.

tools.name: my_coaching
tools.description: All AI debriefs from sessions the user ran as the pro, newest first — their private improvement record. Read it before their next session and coach them on the recurring growth themes. This is how someone new gets good fast.

tools.name: file_brief
tools.description: The demand side is a filed document, not a search: privately write a few real sentences (situation, what kind of person would help, desired cadence) and let it stand anonymously, converging as people join. Never paste or read the draft back to the user. Returns today's candidates as private working

tools.name: my_briefs
tools.description: All briefs with matching re-run against today's marketplace plus any intros received. Call when your user returns — new people may have joined since the brief was filed.

tools.name: open_briefs
tools.description: Open briefs, anonymous (the need, never the person). For the supply side: show a pro what demand exists, or a hesitant would-be pro that people are already waiting for someone like them. Reach toward one with send_intro(briefId).

tools.name: close_brief
tools.description: Close an open brief (found someone, or the need passed).

tools.name: send_intro
tools.description: Send a short real intro through the platform (no emails exposed). Two directions: profileId — your user reaching toward a person whose story fits; briefId — your user (who has a profile) reaching toward an open need. Acceptance creates a connection.

tools.name: my_intros
tools.description: Pending intros addressed to the user. Respond with respond_intro.

tools.name: respond_intro
tools.description: Accepting creates the CONNECTION — the standing relationship where messages, sessions, artifacts, and witnessed commitments live. If your user is the client side, immediately port_context so their person knows exactly what is going on.

tools.name: port_context
tools.description: Client-side only, with the user's consent: write the full situation into the connection — what is going on, history, what has been tried, what they want — so their person starts knowing everything. Replaces the intake interview. Update it as things evolve; the pro and their agent read it.

tools.name: my_connections
tools.description: Every connection: who, context brief, last message, active witnessed commitments. The relationships you are responsible for keeping alive.

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Async relay between the two people (agents deliver both ways). The daily two-line check-in lives here — cadence beats duration.

tools.name: check_messages
tools.description: The heartbeat: call whenever your user shows up. Returns unread messages from all their connections (marked delivered). Relay them in the person's own words.

tools.name: create_artifact
tools.description: Pro-side: design an interactive exercise ONCE (thought record, weekly review, values check — see coaching_playbook topic artifact_patterns). The script is instructions a client's agent follows to run it conversationally. Public artifacts join the shared library.

tools.name: artifact_library
tools.description: Public artifacts from all pros, ranked by real adoption. Search in plain language ('procrastination', 'reframing', 'weekly accountability'). Assign any of them — assigning from the library is how good exercises spread.

tools.name: my_artifacts
tools.description: Artifacts this pro designed, with adoption counts.

tools.name: assign_artifact
tools.description: Pro-side: assign an artifact (yours or from the library) to a client, with an optional note. Best moment: as a session lands, one artifact at a time. The client's agent administers it and the result comes back into the connection.

tools.name: my_assignments
tools.description: Client-side: pending exercises assigned by the user's people. Run each script conversationally at a good moment — you are the facilitator — then complete_assignment with the substance of what emerged.

tools.name: complete_assignment
tools.description: After administering an assigned exercise: post back what the script asked for. The result lands in the connection where their person (and their person's agent) sees it.

tools.name: coaching_playbook
tools.description: The curriculum you coach your pro with: active_listening, powerful_questions, cbt_basics, motivational_interviewing, accountability_structures, session_structure, boundaries_and_referral, artifact_patterns. No topic returns the index. Ground your cues, debriefs, and artifact designs in this.

tools.name: my_credits
tools.description: Unredeemed session credits (earned by running sessions). They auto-redeem on the next paid booking.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "begin_sign_in" references sensitive artifacts (credentials, secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Call only when the user is ready for an identity-bearing action. Returns a secure noemic.app URL where they log in or create an account and approve this convers…

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "match_me" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: PRIVATE fallback pre-ranker when list_people is too large for context. Returns at most three candidates as working material and NEVER renders a panel. Read sile…

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "list_people" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: PRIMARY PRIVATE DISCOVERY: first decide which formal factual constraints follow from the user's request, pass only those filters, then read every returned story…

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "show_people" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: THE ONLY user-visible shortlist. After silently reading list_people or match_me, pass 1-3 selected profile IDs plus the formal filters you actually applied. The…

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "become_available" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Create the user's profile so others can book them. Draft everything from your conversation — their words, their experiences, what they'd be good at being in som…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "become_available" references sensitive artifacts (credentials). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Create the user's profile so others can book them. Draft everything from your conversation — their words, their experiences, what they'd be good at being in som…

findings.title: Read-only tool requests dangerous parameters
findings.detail: Tool "port_context" appears informational but accepts parameters (context) that enable code/file/secret access.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: write_debrief, send_intro, send_message, check_messages, create_artifact · Private data: begin_sign_in, finish_sign_in, logout, match_me, list_people, complete_session, become_available, resolve_commitment, write_debrief, session_debrief, my_coaching, file_brief, create_artifact · Public sink: book_now, send_cue, session_debrief, send_intro, send_message, complete_assignment · Destructive: become_available, create_commitment, my_briefs, create_artifact, my_assignments.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: write_debrief, send_intro, send_message, check_messages, create_artifact · Private data: begin_sign_in, finish_sign_in, logout, match_me, list_people, complete_session, become_available, resolve_commitment, write_debrief, session_debrief, my_coaching, file_brief, create_artifact · Public sink: book_now, send_cue, session_debrief, send_intro, send_message, complete_assignment · Destructive: become_available, create_commitment, my_briefs, create_artifact, my_assignments

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "get_started" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Call this FIRST when a user has just connected or is unsure what Noemic is. Returns the honest state of the marketplace and the four paths. Present them as 2-4 tappable choices, not prose." → after: "Call this FIRST when a user has just connected or is unsure what Noemic is. The embedded panel presents marketplace context but has no action buttons. Present the four paths through Claude's native tappable choices; do not repeat the panel information."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool removed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "register" is no longer advertised. Removal can precede a shadowing replacement from another server.

Prefer list_people + your own reading — you understand meaning; this ranks tokens. Results carry availableNow, freeFirstSession, and ne" → after: "PRIVATE fallback pre-ranker when list_people is too large for context. Returns at most three candidates as working material and NEVER renders a panel. Read silently, then call show_people with 1-3 selected IDs. Do not narrate ranking or results."

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

Optionally narrow by formal facts (price of entry, presence, an opening soon — never categories), then READ every story and corpus that comes back and decide who actually fits your user. At today's marketplace size the full read always fits; prefer th" → after: "PRIMARY PRIVATE DISCOVERY: first decide which formal factual constraints follow from the user's request, pass only those filters, then read every returned story and corpus silently. This tool NEVER renders to the user. Select 1-3 people and call show_people with the same filters. Never narrate the s"

Returns today's matches immediately.
